text:
Market today: Mood is cautious. The real driver for your names is Apple dropping 5% on price hikes from a memory crunch — that's a supply-side chip story that ripples directly into semiconductors and AI hardware. Watch for sympathy weakness across your entire tech/chip book today.

Your holdings: PLTR is your biggest pain point — down 17% and on a SELL signal; on margin that hole gets deeper fast, stop is $85, know your exit. AVGO is your largest position, already down nearly 13%, and a memory crunch headline hits it directly since it's deep in AI chip supply chains — TRIM signal means act, not wait. SPAL is down 17% on a 2x leveraged ETF that decays daily — these bleed even sideways; it's near its $19 stop, be ready to cut. SPCX is brand-new and highly volatile with thin data — margin and IPO chaos are a bad mix.

Watch: 1) How AVGO trades today given the memory/chip supply headline. 2) Whether PLTR holds above $100 — a break there accelerates pressure.

Bottom line: Your margin exposure plus three names bleeding double-digits means today is about cutting losses, not hoping for bounces.
